title: Civil Service Commission v. Ledesma
holding: Defines grave misconduct: misconduct is a transgression of an established rule; it is GRAVE where the elements of corruption, clear intent to violate the law, or flagrant disregard of an established rule are present. Grounds the Grave-Misconduct characterization in Counts I & II.

links.relevance: grave-misconduct standard for the CART-conflict counts

title: Office of the Ombudsman v. Miedes
holding: Applies the grave-misconduct standard (corruption / clear intent / flagrant disregard) and the Ombudsman's disciplinary authority over erring public officers.

title: Vertudes v. Buenaflor
holding: Reiterates the grave-misconduct elements and that flagrant disregard of an established rule elevates misconduct to grave.
